summary: The single-line assertion update in argvShape_exactlySixArgs_knownPositions correctly pins argv[5] to '*:W' while preserving all other invariants (length=6, positions 0-4, --pid=\d+ regex). Litmus test passes: removing or reverting the production token causes the test to fail. The five untouched tests continue to defend AC-L2 (placeholder semantics, timeout, drain-protocol). Tests are deterministic (Robolectric SDK 33, hand-rolled fakes, no Mockito/MockK), and the unit-level argv pin is sufficient given AC-L3 is explicitly deferred to manual Honor 200 QA in Task 12 — adding a 'WARN line passes through' test would just exercise String(stdout, UTF-8) against a faked runner, providing zero additional confidence. One minor finding: the KDoc on LogcatReader.kt is now stale (says 'ERROR level'), but this is not a test issue and does not block approval.

findings.issue: KDoc on LogcatReader still reads 'Reads the current process's own logcat at ERROR level' — stale after the *:E → *:W widening. Not a test concern, but the docstring now contradicts the production behavior the tests pin.
findings.fix: Update the KDoc to say 'at WARN level (and above)'. Out-of-scope for the test-review verdict but cheap to fix in this same task or a follow-up touch-up commit before phase merge.
